steps.label: Intake Member Demographic and Relationship Entry
steps.description: On this screen, you will enter detailed information for each individual involved in the intake process. The form is designed to capture demographic details, relationship to the primary client, contact information, and language preference. Clear instructions are provided for each field, and you can add multiple individuals as needed. Use the 'Search in MCI' button to check if the individual already exists in the Master Client Index before proceeding. Guidance is provided to ensure accurate and complete data entry, and you will be notified of any missing or invalid information before moving forward.

fields.label: Relationship to Primary Client
fields.error_message: Relationship to primary client is required.

fields.label: Contact Phone Number
fields.error_message: Phone number must be valid format.

data_validation: Birth date cannot be in the future.
Cannot have more than one mother or father per intake.
Cannot have duplicate spouse entries for the same individual.
SSN must be unique across all individuals.
Phone number must be valid format.
If minor (under 18), guardian information is mandatory.
If relationship is 'Spouse', age must be at least 18.
Address must be complete and valid.

buttons.label: Search in MCI
buttons.description: Initiates a search for the entered individual in the Master Client Index using the provided demographic details.

buttons.label: Add Another Individual
buttons.description: Allows the user to add another individual to the intake by repeating the demographic and relationship entry section.

buttons.label: Next
buttons.description: Proceeds to the next step after all required information is entered and validated.

steps.label: Master Client Index Search Results
steps.description: After initiating a search in the Master Client Index (MCI), this screen displays any matching individuals found based on the entered demographic criteria. You will see a list of possible matches with key details to help you identify the correct individual. If a match is found, select the individual to proceed. If no match is found, you will be given the option to create a new client record. Clear instructions and guidance are provided to help you make the correct selection and understand the implications of creating a new record.

fields.label: Matching Individuals
fields.error_message: Select a matching individual or proceed to create a new record.
fields.display_field: Full Name, Date of Birth, Gender, SSN

data_validation: If a matching individual is selected, ensure demographic details are consistent.
If no match is found, allow creation of new client record.
SSN must be unique for new records.
Cannot proceed without selecting a match or confirming creation of a new record.

buttons.label: Select Individual
buttons.description: Selects the matching individual from the MCI search results to proceed with intake.

buttons.label: Create New Client Record
buttons.description: Proceeds to create a new client record if no match is found in the MCI.

buttons.label: Back
buttons.description: Returns to the previous screen to edit or update search criteria.

steps.label: Search for Existing Individual in Master Client Index (MCI)
steps.description: This interface is triggered when the Intake Worker clicks the 'Search in MCI' button. It performs a search in the Master Client Index (MCI) using the provided demographic criteria (such as name, date of birth, and social security number). If a matching individual is found, the interface returns the match(es) for user selection. If no match is found, the response indicates that the user may proceed to create a new client record.

error_handling.fallback_behavior: If the API call fails or times out, display an error message to the user and allow manual entry of client data for new record creation.

security.encryption: TLS 1.2+ for all data in transit

steps.label: New Client Record Creation Confirmation
steps.description: This screen confirms the creation of a new client record using the information entered during intake. You will review the details for accuracy and completeness before saving. Instructions are provided to verify all information, and you must acknowledge that the individual does not exist in the Master Client Index. Once confirmed, the new client record will be saved and you can continue the intake process for additional members as needed.

fields.label: Relationship to Primary Client
fields.error_message: Relationship to primary client is required.

fields.label: Contact Phone Number
fields.error_message: Phone number must be valid format.

data_validation: All required fields must be reviewed and confirmed before saving.
SSN must be unique for new records.
Birth date cannot be in the future.
Cannot have more than one mother or father per intake.
Cannot have duplicate spouse entries for the same individual.
If minor (under 18), guardian information is mandatory.

buttons.label: Confirm and Save
buttons.description: Saves the new client record and continues the intake process for additional members as needed.

buttons.label: Back
buttons.description: Returns to the previous screen to edit or update information before saving.

steps.label: Create New Client Record
steps.description: If no matching individual is found in the Master Client Index (MCI), the system must allow the Intake Worker to create a new client record using the information entered on the Intake Member Information Entry screen. The new client record is saved, and the intake process continues for additional members as needed.

rules.label: Allow New Client Record Creation
rules.logic: If the MCI search returns an error status or no matching individual is found, enable the functionality for the Intake Worker to create a new client record using the entered member information.

rules.label: Save New Client Record
rules.logic: Upon creation, save the new client record to the system database and proceed to the next step in the intake process for additional members if required.

rules.label: Handle MCI Connectivity Error
rules.logic: If the MCI search fails due to a connectivity error (e.g., 'Failed to initialize Neo4j connection'), display an appropriate error message to the Intake Worker and allow manual creation of the client record.
